# Gold/Coin Trading Bot — Copilot Instructions

## Language & Communication
- User speaks **Persian (Farsi)**. Respond in Persian for explanations.
- Code, comments, and commit messages in **English**.
- After every fix: **commit with descriptive message + deploy via `make up`**.

## Architecture Overview

### Two-Server Deployment
| Server | Location | Services | Domain |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Foreign** | Germany (current machine) | Bot + API + Sync Worker + DB + Redis | — |
| **Iran** | 87.107.3.22 | API + Nginx + Frontend (no bot) | `coin.gold-trade.ir` |

### Tech Stack
- **Backend**: FastAPI 0.111 + SQLAlchemy 2.0 (async, asyncpg) + PostgreSQL 15 + Redis 7
- **Bot**: aiogram 3.10 (long-polling, FSM)
- **Frontend**: Vue 3 + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS + Vite (PWA)
- **Deploy**: `make up` → `deploy.sh all` (builds frontend, rsyncs to Iran, Docker rebuild both, auto-starts sync workers, runs cross-server recovery)
- **Production recoverability**: `make production-backup-iran` creates DB/Redis/uploads/audit backup artifacts; `make production-recoverability-drill` verifies the DB dump in a temporary PostgreSQL container without touching production.

### Docker Services (Foreign)
`app` (FastAPI:8000), `bot` (aiogram polling), `sync_worker`, `migration` (alembic), `db` (postgres:15), `redis` (redis:7), `adminer` (127.0.0.1:8080)

Iran is identical but **no bot service**. Nginx proxies `/api/` → backend, `/` → SPA (`mini_app_dist/`).

## Database Models

### Core Tables
- **users**: id, account_name (unique), mobile_number (unique), telegram_id (nullable, unique), full_name, address, role (Enum), has_bot_access, is_deleted, deleted_at, trading_restricted_until, max_daily_trades, max_active_commodities, max_daily_requests, limitations_expire_at, trades_count, commodities_traded_count, channel_messages_count, can_block_users, max_blocked_users, last_seen_at
- **invitations**: id, account_name, mobile_number, token (unique), short_code (unique), role, created_by_id→users, is_used, expires_at
- **offers**: id, version_id (optimistic lock), user_id→users, offer_type (buy/sell), commodity_id, quantity, price, remaining_quantity, is_wholesale, lot_sizes (JSON), status (active/completed/cancelled/expired), notes, channel_message_id, idempotency_key
- **trades**: id, version_id, trade_number (unique, starts 10000), offer_id, offer_user_id, offer_user_mobile, responder_user_id, responder_user_mobile, commodity_id, trade_type, quantity, price, status (pending/confirmed/completed/cancelled), idempotency_key
- **messages**: id, sender_id, receiver_id, reply_to_message_id, forwarded_from_id, content, message_type (text/image/sticker), is_read, is_deleted, edit_history (JSON)
- **conversations**: id, user1_id (smaller), user2_id (larger), last_message_id, unread_count_user1/2
- **notifications**: id, user_id, message, is_read, level (INFO/SUCCESS/WARNING/ERROR), category (SYSTEM/USER/TRADE)
- **commodities** + **commodity_aliases**: name/alias with cascade delete
- **user_blocks**: blocker_id, blocked_id (unidirectional data, bidirectional logic)
- **chat_files**: UUID pk, uploader_id, s3_key (disk path), file_name, mime_type, size, thumbnail (base64)
- **trading_settings**: key-value store (offer_expiry_minutes, max_active_offers, etc.)
- **change_log** + **sync_blocks**: Cross-server sync tracking

### Enums
- `UserRole`: WATCH="تماشا", STANDARD="عادی", POLICE="پلیس", MIDDLE_MANAGER="مدیر میانی", SUPER_ADMIN="مدیر ارشد"
- `OfferType`: buy/sell | `OfferStatus`: active/completed/cancelled/expired
- `TradeType`: buy/sell | `TradeStatus`: pending/confirmed/completed/cancelled

## API Endpoints Summary

### Auth (`/api/auth`): request-otp, resend-otp-sms, verify-otp, webapp-login, refresh, register-otp-request/verify/complete, /me
### Invitations (`/api/invitations`): CRUD + lookup/{short_code} + validate/{token}
### Users (`/api/users`): CRUD (Super Admin/Dev Key required). DELETE = soft delete
### Offers (`/api/offers`): CRUD + /parse (NLP). Creates Telegram channel messages
### Trades (`/api/trades`): POST (execute on offer with FOR UPDATE lock), GET my/with/{user_id}
### Chat (`/api/chat`): conversations, messages, send, edit (48h), delete (48h), upload-image, files/{id}, stickers, typing, read, poll
### Notifications (`/api/notifications`): CRUD + SSE stream
### Blocks (`/api/blocks`): block/unblock/check/search
### Realtime (`/api/realtime`): WebSocket + SSE via Redis pub/sub
### Sync (`/api/sync/receive`): HMAC-signed cross-server sync
### Sync Resync (`/api/sync/resync`): POST, Dev API Key, pushes unsynced change_log entries (params: limit, table_filter)
### Config (`/api/config`): Public bot_username + frontend_url

| Handler | Purpose |
|---|---|
| start.py | /start + invitation token, registration FSM (contact→address), channel trade callbacks |
| link_account.py | /link — link web account to Telegram |
| panel.py | User/admin panels, trading settings view/edit |
| trade_create.py | Offer creation FSM: type→commodity→quantity→lots→price→notes→confirm |
| trade_execute.py | Channel inline button → execute trade |
| trade_manage.py | Expire offer from bot |
| trade_history.py | History display, filtering, Excel/PDF export |
| admin.py | Invitation creation FSM |
| admin_commodities.py | Commodity + alias CRUD via bot |
| admin_users.py | User management: list, search, profile, block, limit, role, delete |
| block_manage.py | User-facing block management |

## Frontend Components

### Views: DashboardView, LoginView (OTP), MarketView, MessengerView, ProfileView, AdminView, InviteLanding, WebRegister
### Key Components: UserProfile (admin actions), UserManager (user list), CreateInvitationView, CommodityManager, ChatView, OffersList, TradingView, NotificationCenter, TradingSettings
- **Market runtime UI**: Shared open/closed browser chrome outside `MarketView.vue` must consume `frontend/src/composables/useMarketRuntime.ts`. Keep the bottom navigation market closed label and dashboard market-entry state driven by `/api/trading-settings/market-state` plus `market:opened` / `market:closed` realtime events.
- **Dashboard current-day trades**: `DashboardView.vue` shows the current Iran-day trade summary for both normal users and accountants by calling `/api/trades/my` with identical `from_date` / `to_date` query values. Keep the row columns as counterparty, trade type, commodity, quantity, and unit price; compute accountant trade type from the owner perspective (`accountant_owner_user_id`), not the accountant user id.
- **Trade history ownership**: `actor_user_id` on trades is audit/initiator metadata only. `/api/trades/my`, `/api/trades/with/{user_id}`, direct trade-history access checks, and dashboard history rows must be based on actual economic participants (`offer_user_id` or `responder_user_id`) so customer-mediated chain legs are not shown as trades for the actor-only customer.
- **Note on Chat Components**: `ChatView.vue` acts as the data orchestrator (WebSockets, IndexedDB, API polling) and delegates UI rendering to 4 extracted subcomponents: `ChatHeader.vue`, `ChatInputBar.vue`, `ChatMessageItem.vue`, and `ChatContextMenu.vue`. When modifying chat features, ensure state management stays in `ChatView.vue` while UI/gesture logic goes into the respective subcomponent.
- **Messenger old-message pagination**: Direct/group/channel history uses `loadOlderMessages()` with `before_id`; `ChatView.vue` intentionally starts loading older pages before the user reaches the absolute top via a bounded viewport-based threshold, while preserving the prepend scroll anchor. Do not revert this to a tiny top-only trigger.

### Auth Flow (`utils/auth.ts`)
- Tokens in `localStorage`: `auth_token`, `refresh_token`
- `apiFetch()` wrapper: adds Bearer token, auto-retries 401 with refresh
- `setupExpiryTimer()`: checks every 30s, auto-refreshes before expiry
- `apiBaseUrl = import.meta.env.VITE_API_BASE_URL || ''` → **empty string = same-origin** (Nginx proxies /api/)

## Critical Patterns & Gotchas

### Authentication
- JWT Subject = `user.id` (integer). Access token 60min, refresh 30 days, HS256.
- `get_current_user()` tries ID first, fallback to telegram_id (legacy).
- Admin endpoints use `verify_super_admin_or_dev_key` dependency.
- DEV_API_KEY: `3f7bc530-b8a8-4125-9b79-256dc46bc530` (header: `X-Dev-Api-Key`)
- **Session authority**: `users.home_server` is shared through normal user sync, but `user_sessions` / `session_login_requests` remain local security state. Before creating OTP/SMS/login state on a non-home server, call the signed `/api/sessions/internal/authority-check` path through `core.session_authority`; if the home server reports active sessions, fail with the active-session message and do not send OTP/SMS.

### Soft Delete
Users: `is_deleted=True, deleted_at=datetime.utcnow()`. Atomic: preserves mobiles in trades, expires offers, deletes invitations.

### apiBaseUrl = '' (Empty String)
Frontend `apiBaseUrl` is `''` for same-origin. **NEVER use falsy checks** like `if (!apiBaseUrl)` — empty string is falsy in JS but is the correct value.

### Two-Server Sync
SQLAlchemy event listeners → `change_log` + Redis queue + direct HTTP push. Sync worker retries. Receiver does dependency-ordered upserts. FK violations deferred and retried.

### Real-time
WebSocket + SSE via Redis pub/sub. Events: `offer:created/expired/updated/cancelled/completed`, `trade:created`. Per-user events via `notifications:{user_id}` channel.

### Optimistic Locking
Offers and Trades use `version_id`. SQLAlchemy raises `StaleDataError` on conflict.

### Persian/Jalali
Store UTC → display Iran time (Asia/Tehran) + Jalali calendar. Persian numeral normalization on all inputs.

### SMS (SMS.ir)
- Plain SMS uses SMS.ir REST `POST /v1/send/bulk` with `X-API-KEY`, `lineNumber`, `messageText`, and `mobiles`.
- OTP SMS uses SMS.ir Verify `POST /v1/send/verify` when `SMSIR_OTP_TEMPLATE_ID` is configured; otherwise it falls back to plain SMS.
- Required env: `SMSIR_API_KEY`, `SMSIR_LINE_NUMBER`. Optional Verify env: `SMSIR_OTP_TEMPLATE_ID`, `SMSIR_OTP_TEMPLATE_PARAMETER` (default `Code`).
- Invitation SMS is temporarily web-only and role-worded: general invitations say `معامله گر`, accountant invitations say `حسابدار`, and customer invitations say `مشتری`. Send only the direct `/register?token=...` link and do not include Telegram text, names, or `t.me` links in the SMS body. Keep it under 3 UCS-2 segments (~161 chars).
- Melipayamak is not wired into production yet. Use `scripts/melipayamak_probe.py` for isolated manual tests; pass credentials via env (`MELIPAYAMAK_USERNAME`, `MELIPAYAMAK_API_KEY` or `MELIPAYAMAK_PASSWORD`, `MELIPAYAMAK_FROM`) and do not commit provider secrets.

## Core Services

| Service | Purpose |
|---|---|
| config.py | pydantic-settings: bot_token, server_mode, db urls, jwt_secret, etc. |
| db.py | Async SQLAlchemy engine (pool_size=100, max_overflow=50) |
| security.py | JWT create/verify (access + refresh tokens) |
| cache.py | Redis cache: users (5min), commodities (5min), offer counts (30s) |
| events.py | SQLAlchemy after_insert/update/delete → sync + real-time publish |
| sync_push.py | HMAC-signed HTTP push to other server (thread pool) |
| sync_worker.py | Redis queue consumer, batch + retry sync |
| offer_expiry.py | Background task: expire old offers every 15s |
| notifications.py | Cross-server notification delivery |
| sms.py | SMS.ir integration |
| connectivity.py | Iran server: checks Telegram API accessibility every 30s |
| trading_settings.py | Dynamic settings: Redis cache → DB → JSON → defaults |

## Media & File Storage
- **Backend Storage**: Local disk (migrated from S3). Max size configured to 50MB in Nginx (`client_max_body_size`) and FastAPI. 
- **Upload Endpoint**: `/api/chat/upload-media` (Accepts `image/*` and `video/*`).
- **Download Endpoint**: `/api/chat/files/{id}?token=`
- **Frontend Uploads**: Uses `XMLHttpRequest` instead of `fetch` to accurately track `upload_progress`.
- **Frontend Downloads**: Uses `fetch` with `ReadableStream` to track `download_progress`. Media is NOT auto-downloaded on load (saves bandwidth).
- **Frontend Caching**: Uses `IndexedDB` (`trading_bot_images` store) to cache media blobs locally. A base64 `thumbnail` is shipped with the DB message to show a blurred preview while the full file downloads.
- **UI Rendering Rule**: Always render `<img>` or `<video>` tags unconditionally using `local_blob_url` or `imageCache` when available. Progress rings should be placed as an absolute overlay *on top* of the media, rather than using `v-else-if` to hide the media tag conditionally (which causes the container to collapse).

## Deploy Commands
```bash
make up          # Full deploy (both servers)
make frontend    # Frontend only
make iran        # Iran server only
make foreign     # Foreign server only
make logs        # Docker logs (foreign)
make logs-iran   # Docker logs (iran)
make restart     # Restart containers (foreign)
make status      # Container status
```

## Known Fixed Issues (Don't Reintroduce)
1. **apiBaseUrl falsy check**: `!props.apiBaseUrl` blocks all admin actions because `'' == falsy`
2. **soft_delete() missing**: User model needs `soft_delete()` method
3. **Bot hard delete**: Must soft-delete, not `session.delete()` (FK violations)
4. **bot_username null on Iran**: Fixed — `BOT_USERNAME=mbmtrading1_bot` added to Iran `.env`. Was missing, causing `https://t.me/None?start=...` links in invitations and SMS.
5. **Token expiry mismatch**: Access 60min, refresh 30 days — keep consistent
6. **OTP not deleted after verify**: Must delete Redis OTP key after successful verification
7. **SMS too long**: Keep invitation SMS under 3 UCS-2 segments (~161 chars)
8. **Admin user list showing only active users**: GET /api/users defaults `include_deleted=False` so only active users are shown. Bot also filters `is_deleted==False`. Deleted users will be viewable later via a dedicated admin section.
9. **Sync data not reaching other server**: change_log entries created by events outside SQLAlchemy (direct SQL, scripts) have no sync entries. Use `POST /api/sync/resync` endpoint (Dev API Key required) to push unsynced change_log entries in batches of 50. Real-time sync via events.py works for normal app operations.

## Assistant Collaboration Protocol

### Multi-Assistant Coordination
- **Ownership**: Each assistant (Antigravity, Copilot, etc.) MUST only commit changes they have personally verified and initiated. Do not commit code generated by another assistant unless you have fully reviewed and integrated it.
- **Search This File First**: Before any code change, debugging task, or new feature, search this `copilot-instructions.md` file for task keywords, related file names, component names, routes, services, and recent change descriptions. Use those matches to identify relevant constraints, prior fixes, regressions, and nearby files before editing.
- **Context Awareness**: Before starting a major task, check the **Major Changes History** below and the `brain/` directory for recent implementation plans and walkthroughs.

### Commit Rules
- After every code/config/docs change, update this file with a complete but concise change report before committing. Prefer one Major Changes History row plus any nearby operational rule updates needed to prevent regressions.
- Commit messages must clearly state the scope of changes.
- If multiple assistants are working sequentially, ensure each step is committed separately to maintain a clean git history and avoid "ghost" changes.

| 2026-04-22 18:30 UTC | Copilot | **Chat Upload Phase 1 - Background Upload Service**: Fixed critical bug where navigating away from the messenger or reloading the app cancelled in-progress media uploads. Created a module-level singleton service `frontend/src/services/chatUploadBackground.ts` that owns the upload lifecycle (XHR + /chat/send + album batching) independent of Vue component lifecycle, persists pending uploads to IndexedDB (`chat_upload_queue`/`pending` with stored Blob), captures the receiver `userId` at submit time instead of reading `selectedUserId.value` later, and emits `added`/`progress`/`uploaded`/`sent`/`error`/`cancelled` events via EventTarget. Refactored `useChatMedia.ts` to delegate XHR+send to the service after local EXIF/compression preprocessing and subscribe to events for reactive UI patching of currently-visible messages. `useChatMessages.ts`/`loadMessages` now grafts service-pending uploads back onto the messages list so re-entering a chat while uploads are in flight (or after a reload) shows them again. `App.vue` initializes the service on mount which also resumes persisted queued/uploading/uploaded pending items from IndexedDB. |
| 2026-04-22 19:15 UTC | Copilot | **Chat Upload Phase 1.1 - Concurrency Gate & Auto-Retry**: Fixed three user-reported regressions after Phase 1 when uploading 10-15 image albums: (1) market page slowdown, (2) 2-3 images failing per album, (3) first text message to another user not appearing until navigating back. Root cause: service fired an XHR per media item, saturating the browser's ~6 per-origin connection pool and blocking unrelated `/api/*` calls including `/api/chat/send`, `/api/chat/poll`, and `/api/offers`. Added `MAX_CONCURRENT_UPLOADS = 2` gate with a FIFO `uploadQueue` and `pumpUploadQueue()` in `frontend/src/services/chatUploadBackground.ts`, routed both `submitUpload` and resume paths through `enqueueUpload`, and added automatic exponential-backoff retry (up to 3 attempts) for transient network / 5xx errors via `isTransientUploadError` + `retryCount` field on `PendingUpload`. This preserves connection slots for text message sends and other API traffic while a large album uploads in the background. |
| 2026-04-22 20:00 UTC | Copilot | **Chat Upload Phase 1.2 - Offload Pillow to Thread Pool**: Fixed backend event-loop stall during multi-image uploads that caused all other `/api/chat/*` requests (messages, conversations, poll) to queue behind the upload handler. The `upload-media` endpoint in `api/routers/chat.py` called `PIL.Image.open / ImageOps.exif_transpose / pil_img.save` synchronously inside an async handler, blocking the single uvicorn worker's event loop for ~100-500ms per image. With the Phase 1.1 frontend concurrency of 2, the event loop was effectively saturated for the entire album duration, making the messenger slow (chat history load, optimistic text display) even though the unrelated market endpoint stayed fast. Wrapped the Pillow block in `asyncio.to_thread(...)` so CPU-bound EXIF rotation and re-encoding runs in the default thread pool and the event loop stays free to service concurrent chat API requests. |
| 2026-04-22 20:45 UTC | Copilot | **Chat Upload Phase 1.3 - Multi-Worker Backend & libmagic Offload**: After Phase 1.2 improved chat responsiveness during uploads but did not eliminate it, scaled the backend to use two uvicorn workers so a second event loop can service `/api/chat/*` reads while the first is processing upload Pillow/DB work. (1) `docker-compose.yml`: `uvicorn main:app --workers 2`. (2) `core/db.py`: reduced `pool_size=40, max_overflow=20` per worker — total ~120 connections across 2 workers, well within the existing `max_connections=500` in the PG container. (3) `api/routers/chat.py`: offloaded CPU-bound `magic.from_buffer` libmagic probe to `asyncio.to_thread` so the upload handler no longer blocks the event loop on file type detection either. (4) Host: swap grown from 1GB to 4GB as a safety margin for the extra worker memory footprint. Server has 7.6GB RAM (not 4GB) which is comfortable for 2 workers; single-server deploy only so no Iran side changes. |
| 2026-04-22 21:30 UTC | Copilot | **Chat Upload Phase 1.4 - Album Stall Safety Net**: Fixed a reproducible bug where switching chats mid-upload could leave the second album permanently stuck with all 13 progress circles at 100% but zero `/chat/send` dispatches. Root cause: (a) `runUpload` set no `xhr.timeout`, so a half-dead connection after full byte upload left the XHR hanging forever with `phase='uploading'`, making `hasStillUploading` true forever inside `flushAlbumBatchIfReady`; (b) if preprocessing silently dropped one file before `submitUpload`, `optimisticIds.size` stayed below `expectedCount` forever. Fixes in `frontend/src/services/chatUploadBackground.ts`: (1) Added `xhr.timeout = 5min` + `xhr.ontimeout` that rejects as a transient error so the existing retry path kicks in. (2) Added `forceFlushStalledAlbums()` safety net, triggered from `runUploadWithGate` finally whenever the pipeline fully drains (active=0, queue empty), plus a 30s periodic watchdog started in `initChatUploadBackground`. Stalled batches whose submitted count is below their expected count are rescued by recomputing `expectedCount` down to the actual submitted count. |
| 2026-04-23 07:15 UTC | Copilot | **Chat Upload Phase 1.5 - Preprocess Hang Safety Net**: Fixed a reproducible bug where large album media uploads would get stuck forever in the "preparing" phase without the progress circle ever advancing. Root cause: mobile browsers (Safari iOS, Chrome Android on low-RAM devices) silently kill a Web Worker when its OffscreenCanvas memory footprint grows during batch image preprocess, and neither `onmessage` nor `onerror` fires. The worker slot's `busy` flag stays true forever, and the composable's `activePreprocessCount` never decrements, locking the entire preprocess pipeline for the rest of the album. (1) Added 60s per-job timeout with `armSlotTimeout`/`clearSlotTimeout` inside `frontend/src/utils/imagePreprocessClient.ts` that terminates the dead worker, recreates the slot, rejects the stuck job, and resumes `dispatchNext` so the pool keeps draining. (2) Added a defense-in-depth 90s outer timeout in `launchPreprocessJob` inside `frontend/src/composables/chat/useChatMedia.ts` so even non-worker preprocess paths (main-thread fallback, video metadata probe, thumbnail generation) can never leak `activePreprocessCount` if their underlying promise hangs. |
| 2026-04-23 07:22 UTC | Copilot | **Chat Messenger Perf Phase 8 - shared IntersectionObserver for media hydration**: Introduced `frontend/src/utils/sharedVisibilityObserver.ts`, a singleton `IntersectionObserver` keyed to the media hydration thresholds (900px rootMargin, 0.01 threshold). Refactored `ChatMessageItem.vue` to register its target element through the shared observer instead of instantiating its own per-component `IntersectionObserver`. In chats with many media messages this cuts live observers from N (one per media cell) to 1, reducing background intersection calculation cost on weak Android devices. API remains one-shot: once a cell fires, it's unobserved automatically and the `WeakMap<Element, callback>` entry is cleared. |
| 2026-04-23 08:30 UTC | Copilot | **Chat Upload Phase 1.6 - /chat/send Retry & Broader Transient Detection**: User reported that after Phase 1.5 resolved the "preparing" stall, parallel 3-chat × 10+ image album tests still lost 2 images per session with a failed bubble, despite backend logs showing every `/upload-media` AND `/chat/send` returning 200 OK (so the failure was purely browser-side — a fetch() that hit a closed keep-alive socket in the saturated per-origin connection pool). Root cause: (1) `sendOne()` had no retry path — any single transient `/chat/send` failure marked the media as permanently failed even though the upload blob was already on the server. (2) `isTransientUploadError` regex only matched "network error" / 5xx status codes and missed fetch-style errors like `TypeError: Failed to fetch` (Chromium/Safari), `NetworkError when attempting to fetch` (Firefox), and `Load failed` (iOS Safari). Fixes in `frontend/src/services/chatUploadBackground.ts`: (1) Added `sendRetryCount` field on `PendingUpload` plus `MAX_SEND_RETRIES = 3` cap. (2) `sendOne()` catch branch now checks `isTransientUploadError` first and, if transient, reverts `phase` to `'uploaded'`, persists via `idbPut`, and schedules another `sendOne` after exponential backoff (shared `computeRetryDelayMs` with upload-media retries). (3) Broadened `isTransientUploadError` to recognize `Failed to fetch`, `NetworkError`, `Load failed`, `connection was lost`, and the Persian variant `خطای ارسال (5xx/408)`. |
| 2026-04-23 09:05 UTC | Copilot | **Chat Upload Phase 1.7 - Generous /chat/send Retry Budget**: User reported that after Phase 1.6 parallel 3-chat album tests still lost 1 image in User A and 1 image in User C. Backend logs confirmed all `/upload-media` and `/chat/send` returned 200 OK, so failures were purely browser-side transient fetch() hiccups on the saturated per-origin connection pool. Phase 1.6's 3-retry budget (1s/2s/4s backoff) was too conservative — with 3 concurrent chats × ~12 images the browser pool stays saturated for tens of seconds, easily exhausting 3 short retries. Since the uploaded blob is ALREADY on the server by the time `/chat/send` is being retried, the retry cost is just a tiny JSON POST. Raised `MAX_SEND_RETRIES` from 3 to 10 and added a dedicated `computeSendRetryDelayMs()` with an exponential backoff capped at 15s (1s → 2s → 4s → 8s → 15s → 15s …) plus wider ±500ms jitter. Upload-media retries stay on the original ~4s-capped schedule since re-uploading is expensive. |
| 2026-04-23 09:33 UTC | Copilot | **Chat Open Warm Cache & Stale Load Guard**: Updated `useChatMessages.ts` to keep a small per-conversation snapshot cache so recently opened chats render immediately from memory while a fresh `/chat/messages/{user_id}` request continues in the background. Also added request-order guards so rapidly switching chats no longer lets an older response overwrite the currently active conversation, while pending background uploads are still merged into the visible timeline. |

| 2026-04-26 09:35 UTC | Copilot | **Image Editor Load Robustness Fix**: Fixed gallery-picked image showing the raw `alt="editing"` text instead of the actual image inside `ImageEditorModal.vue`. Cropper.js was initialized in `onMounted` regardless of whether the `<img>` had successfully loaded the blob URL, and its default `checkOrientation: true` ran an XHR against the blob URL that could fail silently on some mobile browsers, leaving the editor non-functional. Now Cropper class is preloaded in `onMounted` but instantiation is deferred until the `<img>` fires `@load`. Added `@error` handler that displays a localized Persian fallback message instead of the alt text. Also disabled `checkOrientation` and `checkCrossOrigin` since EXIF rotation is already handled both server-side (Pillow) and client-side (createImageBitmap). Cleared the misleading `alt="editing"` placeholder. |

| 2026-04-27 07:18 UTC | Copilot | **Lightbox 3D Context Disengagement Fix (Root Cause)**: After multiple narrower fixes, traced the persistent "image opens zoomed into a corner" bug to the lightbox stage 3D rendering context itself. `.lightbox-stage-scene` had `perspective: 1800px` and `.lightbox-stage-track` had `transform-style: preserve-3d` permanently, while the active stage card and scene received no-op `translate3d(0,0,0)` identity transforms at rest. Together these promoted the opened image to a 3D-projected layer even with no real animation, which on some browsers/devices rendered non-standard crops as a zoomed slice of the image. Removed the identity transforms on the active card and the scene, made `perspective`/`preserve-3d` conditional via a new `is-3d-active` class that only engages during real horizontal album swipes, and kept the resting open state in plain 2D rendering. The horizontal album swipe carousel still uses 3D when actually swiping. |

| 2026-04-27 07:34 UTC | Copilot | **Lightbox Grid-Track Overflow Fix (True Root Cause)**: After the 3D-context fix did not resolve the persistent "image opens zoomed into a corner" bug, traced the real CSS root cause. `.lightbox-stage-card` used `display: grid; place-items: center` with default auto-sized tracks. CSS Grid's auto track sizes against the child's `max-content`/intrinsic dimensions, so for non-standard crops the auto track grew to the image's intrinsic width/height. That made `max-width: 100%` / `max-height: 100%` on the `<img>` resolve against the now-image-sized track instead of the card's fixed box, the image rendered at intrinsic size, and the parent `.lightbox-stage`'s `overflow: hidden` clipped everything except a corner of it — exactly the reported "zoomed corner" behavior. Standard-aspect images happened to fit so the bug was invisible there. Switched `.lightbox-stage-card` to `display: flex; align-items/justify-content: center` with `min-width/min-height: 0`, and added explicit `min-width/min-height: 0; flex: 0 1 auto` on `.lightbox-media`, so the image is reliably constrained to the card's fixed box and letterboxed via `object-fit: contain`. |
| 2026-04-27 07:45 UTC | Copilot | **Sticker Picker Browser-Driven Smooth Swap (env(keyboard-inset-height))**: Replaced the JS-driven spacer/timeout dance with a CSS-native solution. Added `interactive-widget=resizes-content` to the viewport meta in `frontend/index.html`. Updated `EmojiStickerPicker.vue` to accept a CSS expression as `panelHeight`. Refactored `ChatInputBar.vue` so the picker stays mounted throughout both swap directions, with its height computed as `max(0px, calc(target - env(keyboard-inset-height, 0px)))`. The browser now animates `env(keyboard-inset-height)` smoothly in lock-step with the keyboard slide, so the picker exactly fills the keyboard slot frame-by-frame and the input row's Y position stays mathematically constant during the swap. JS spacer kept only as a fallback for browsers without env() support. |

PDF, common images, mp4/webm, mp3/wav, txt/json/xml/csv/md/html), and falls back to the OS share sheet for binary formats like xlsx/heic/zip — never an anchor download — so HEIC/xlsx/txt no longer re-trigger the system download manager on every tap. | 2026-05-09 17:30 UTC | Copilot | **Blank White Page on First Incognito Load Fixed**: Root cause was Vue Router's lazy-loaded route components (LoginView etc.) needing a network fetch before the router's first navigation completes — until then `RouterView` renders nothing → blank white screen. Also removed redundant `window.location.reload()` from `onNeedRefresh` (autoUpdate handles reloads itself) and increased the blank-screen recovery timer from 3s to 10s to avoid false-positive loops on slow connections. |
| 2026-05-09 18:20 UTC | Copilot | **Cold-Load HTML Boot Loader + Eager Login Route Added**: Hardened first-load login boot by adding an inline no-CSS/no-JS boot overlay in `frontend/index.html` with a timeout refresh action, keeping it visible until the first route is actually ready, and changing `/login` from a lazy-loaded route to a static import so new/incognito sessions no longer depend on a separate LoginView chunk before anything can render. |
| 2026-05-09 19:06 UTC | Copilot | **Foreign Nginx Asset Proxy Hotfix Added**: Traced the VS Code integrated-browser login stall to `ERR_CONTENT_LENGTH_MISMATCH` on the main hashed JS asset. Root cause was the active foreign host Nginx proxying `/assets/` through FastAPI and hitting proxy temp-file read failures, truncating JS only for Electron/Chromium clients. Added a reusable `scripts/setup_foreign_nginx.sh` that configures `coin.362514.ir` with a dedicated `/assets/` proxy branch using `proxy_buffering off`, `proxy_request_buffering off`, `proxy_max_temp_file_size 0`, and `gzip off`, matching the live hotfix that restored the login page and install prompt in the integrated browser. |
| 2026-05-09 19:10 UTC | Copilot | **Legacy Browser Login Boot Compatibility Added**: After the inline boot loader exposed repeated timeout on some incognito/mobile browsers, lowered the frontend modern build target from `es2020` to `es2018`, added `@vitejs/plugin-legacy` for older engines, and shortened the boot overlay copy to `در حال بارگذاری`. The measured bottleneck is broad API/session/query latency across market, Messenger, profile, relation, and admin read surfaces; L11 remains pending with recommended outcome `release_ready_with_limits`. |
